mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate-hike instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or predictive t-statistic falls below 1.5 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) or returns are negative in >50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and higher corporate output, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy during early-cycle phases or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price indices exceeding the 310 threshold indicate persistent inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >65% of threshold-crossing months or predictive power vanishes after 2023.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates confirm a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profits, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint effect becomes statistically insignificant or negative returns dominate in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate earnings potential, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and broader economic deceleration, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive accuracy after 2022.
